channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Strategic Investment Solutions Inc.
item.description:
Strategic Investment Solutions Inc. reported a state registry affecting - records in ORLAND PARK, IL.
📊 Records Affected: - | 📍 Location: ORLAND PARK, IL | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: SpecterOps Launches BloodHound Scentry to Accelerate the Practice of Identity Attack Path Management
item.description:
Drawing on years of adversary tradecraft, SpecterOps experts work alongside customers to analyze and eliminate attack paths, protect critical assets, and stay ahead of emerging threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Booz Allen Announces General Availability of Vellox Reverser to Automate Malware Defense
item.description:
The AI-powered product delivers expert-grade malware analysis and reverse engineering in minutes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ivanti EPMM Zero-Day Bugs Spark Exploit Frenzy — Again
item.description:
It's time to phase out the "patch and pray" approach, eliminate needless public interfaces, and enforce authentication controls, one expert says.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Agents 'Swarm,' Security Complexity Follows Suit
item.description:
As AI deployments scale and start to include packs of agents autonomously working in concert, organizations face a naturally amplified attack surface.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nation-State Hackers Put Defense Industrial Base Under Siege
item.description:
Espionage groups from China, Russia and other nations burned at least two dozen zero-days in edge devices in attempts to infiltrate defense contractors' networks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Under Pressure to Bolster Defenses for BYOVD Attacks
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting security gaps to weaponize Windows drivers and terminate security processes in targeted networks, and there may be no easy fixes in sight.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ClickFix Attacks Abuses DNS Lookup Command to Deliver ModeloRAT
item.description:
ClickFix campaigns have adapted to the latest defenses with a new technique to trick users into infecting their own machines with malware.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: RMM Abuse Explodes as Hackers Ditch Malware
item.description:
Remote monitoring and management (RMM) software offers hackers multiple benefits, including stealth, persistence, and operational efficiency.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Poland Energy Survives Attack on Wind, Solar Infrastructure
item.description:
Russia-aligned groups are probable culprits behind the wiper attacks against renewable energy farms, a manufacturer, and a heating and power plant.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Embeds Malware in Android Devices
item.description:
Keenadu downloads payloads that hijack browser searches, commit ad fraud, and execute other actions without user knowledge.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Singapore & Its 4 Major Telcos Fend Off Chinese Hackers
item.description:
After detecting a zero-day attack, the country's effective response was attributed to the tight relationship between its government and private industry.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Android 17 Beta Strengthens Secure-by-Default Design for Privacy and App Security
item.description:
The latest Android version continues to improve security and privacy, according to its developers. The post Android 17 Beta Strengthens Secure-by-Default Design for Privacy and App Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dior, Louis Vuitton, Tiffany Fined $25 Million in South Korea After Data Breaches
item.description:
Luxury brands were among the dozens of major companies whose Salesforce instances were targeted by Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters. The post Dior, Louis Vuitton, Tiffany Fined $25 Million in South Korea After Data Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Password Managers Vulnerable to Vault Compromise Under Malicious Server
item.description:
Researchers at ETH Zurich have tested the security of Bitwarden, LastPass, Dashlane, and 1Password password managers. The post Password Managers Vulnerable to Vault Compromise Under Malicious Server appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 3 Threat Groups Started Targeting ICS/OT in 2025: Dragos
item.description:
Industrial cybersecurity firm Dragos has published its 9th Year in Review OT/ICS Cybersecurity Report. The post 3 Threat Groups Started Targeting ICS/OT in 2025: Dragos appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Man Linked to Phobos Ransomware Arrested in Poland
item.description:
Polish police said they found evidence of cybercrime on the 47-year-old suspect’s devices. The post Man Linked to Phobos Ransomware Arrested in Poland appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyber Insights 2026: The Ongoing Fight to Secure Industrial Control Systems
item.description:
As nation-state actors, ransomware groups, and aging infrastructure collide, organizations must rethink how they defend critical operations through resilience, visibility, and modern security strategies. The post Cyber Insights 2026: The Ongoing Fight to Secure Industrial Control Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: API Threats Grow in Scale as AI Expands the Blast Radius
item.description:
New research shows attackers increasingly abusing APIs at machine speed as AI-driven systems widen exposure and amplify impact. The post API Threats Grow in Scale as AI Expands the Blast Radius appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Offer to Sell Millions of Eurail User Records
item.description:
Eurail has confirmed that the stolen data is up for sale, but it’s still trying to determine how many individuals are impacted. The post Hackers Offer to Sell Millions of Eurail User Records appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: VulnCheck Raises $25 Million in Series B Funding to Scale Vulnerability Intelligence
item.description:
The latest funding round was led by Sorenson Capital and brings the total investment to $45 million. The post VulnCheck Raises $25 Million in Series B Funding to Scale Vulnerability Intelligence appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers target supporters of Iran protests in new espionage campaign
item.description:
The campaign began in early January, shortly after mass nationwide demonstrations erupted across Iran calling for an end to the Islamic Republic system.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canada Goose says leaked customer transaction data did not come from company systems
item.description:
On Saturday afternoon, the ShinyHunters cybercriminal organization claimed to have stolen more than 600,000 records from the company containing personal information.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Polish police detain alleged cybercriminal with Phobos ransomware ties
item.description:
A 47-year-old man was arrested by Polish police for his alleged involvement with the Phobos ransomware operation.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dismantling Defenses: Trump 2.0 Cyber Year in Review
item.description:
The Trump administration has pursued a staggering range of policy pivots this past year that threaten to weaken the nation’s ability and willingness to address a broad spectrum of technology challenges, from cybersecurity and privacy to countering disinformation, fraud and corruption. These shifts, along with the president’s efforts to restrict free speech and freedom of the press, have come at such a rapid clip that many readers probably aren’t even aware of them all.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
item.description:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Healthcare Sector Most Targeted by Ransomware Groups as Attacks Increase 49% YOY
item.description:
A new record was set for ransomware attacks last year, with disclosed ransomware attacks increasing by 49% year-over-year to a […] The post Healthcare Sector Most Targeted by Ransomware Groups as Attacks Increase 49% YOY appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Texas Attorney General Launches Investigation into Conduent Business Services Data Breach
item.description:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has announced that his office has launched an investigation into the data breach at Conduent […] The post Texas Attorney General Launches Investigation into Conduent Business Services Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Emergency Medical Services Authority & Compassion Health Care Settle Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
Emergency Medical Services Authority in Oklahoma and Compassion Health Care in North Carolina were sued over cyberattacks and data breaches. […] The post Emergency Medical Services Authority & Compassion Health Care Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: 2025 Healthcare Data Breach Report
item.description:
More than 700 healthcare data breaches affecting 500 or more individuals are being reported to the Department of Health and […] The post 2025 Healthcare Data Breach Report appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breach Settlements Agreed by Centrelake Medical Group & Des Moines Orthopaedic Surgeons
item.description:
Class action lawsuits over data breaches at Centrelake Medical Group and Des Moines Orthopaedic Surgeons have been resolved with settlements. […] The post Data Breach Settlements Agreed by Centrelake Medical Group & Des Moines Orthopaedic Surgeons appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Do your Staff need Training on HIPAA in Emergency Situations?
item.description:
Emergencies in healthcare are not limited to extreme weather, wildfires, or other natural disasters. Today’s most disruptive incidents are just […] The post Do your Staff need Training on HIPAA in Emergency Situations? appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Carespring Health Care Management & LifeBridge Health Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
item.description:
Carespring Health Care Management in Ohio and LifeBridge Health in Maryland have agreed to settle class action lawsuits stemming from […] The post Carespring Health Care Management & LifeBridge Health Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: March 2, 2025: Deadline for Electronic Submission of 2025 Workplace Injury and Illness Data
item.description:
The deadline for submitting electronic workplace injury and illness information to the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) is March […] The post March 2, 2025: Deadline for Electronic Submission of 2025 Workplace Injury and Illness Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Community Health Action of Staten Island (A part of Sun River Health)
item.description:

🎭 Threat Actor: GENESIS | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: New “Kurd Hackers Forum” Focuses on Middle Eastern Data Breaches and Leaks
item.description:
Reza abasi notes that there is a new forum called the “Kurd Hacker Forum” that focuses on databreaches in Iran, Syria, and Turkey. The domain was registered January 28, 2026. The forum, which is on the clear net, looks like it has the same format as the classic BreachForums, with the same types of sections... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cyber Attacks on Schools Plateaued in 2025, but More Records Exposed
item.description:
Abby Sourwine reports: Ransomware attacks against schools and universities held relatively steady in 2025, but the scale of data exposure rose sharply, driven in part by third-party software vulnerabilities and a handful of outsized higher education breaches. According to U.K.-based technology research company Comparitech’s latest education ransomware roundup, ransomware gangs globally claimed 251 attacks on educational... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Guernsey medical practice sanctioned after cyber criminals access patient data through email account
item.description:
itv reports: Guernsey’s Data Protection Authority (ODPA) has sanctioned First Contact Health after it failed to implement sufficient security measures to prevent a phishing attack. The cybersecurity breach saw fraudsters successfully target an employee’s email account, gaining access to confidential health data at the medical practice. First Contact Health became aware and reported the data breach... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Nevada unveils new statewide data classification policy months after cyberattack
item.description:
Eric Neugeboren reports: Nevada’s IT agency has rolled out a new policy aimed at standardizing the privacy of state data, months after a massive cyberattack crippled certain systems for weeks. The policy announced Wednesday from the Governor’s Technology Office marks the first time the state will have clear-cut categories for data sensitivity. Officials said this will allow... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: He tried to extort the Dutch police. It didn’t work out well for him.
item.description:
He wanted something in return for returning files to the Dutch police. What he got in return was an arrest. A press release from Dutch police sums it up: On Thursday evening around 7:00 PM, police arrested a 40-year-old man from Ridderkerk on Prinses Beatrixstraat in Ridderkerk for computer hacking. Due to a police error,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Marietta also affected by BridgePay ransomware attack.
item.description:
Marietta, Georgia is one of numerous entities affected by the BridgePay ransomware attack. On February 13, the city posted the following notice on its website: The City of Marietta is currently unable to process certain online credit card payments due to a recent service disruption by one of the City’s payment gateway providers. We are... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Montana hospital restores phones as cyber-related network disruptions persist
item.description:
Dysruption Hub reports: Livingston HealthCare in Livingston, Montana, says its phone system has been restored after a cybersecurity incident disrupted communications and led the hospital to take some systems offline, but network disruptions continue as restoration work proceeds. The nonprofit hospital said Feb. 13 that a “potential cybersecurity incident” disrupted its phone systems and network and that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Poland arrests suspect linked to Phobos ransomware operation
item.description:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: Polish police have detained a 47-year-old man suspected of ties to the Phobos ransomware group and seized computers and mobile phones containing stolen credentials, credit card numbers, and server access data. Officers from Poland’s Central Bureau of Cybercrime Control (CBZC) arrested the suspect in the Małopolska region in a joint operation involving... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Security Lapse at MYH: Private Agency Fined After Viral Video
item.description:
Devdiscourse reports: A private security firm faced a fine of Rs 25,000 [USD $275.77] on Tuesday after a viral video allegedly revealed neglect, as two women carried a patient on a stretcher out of Government Maharaja Yashwantrao Chikitsalaya (MYH), officials stated. Considered one of the largest state-operated hospitals in Madhya Pradesh, MYH is under the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hackers Offer to Sell Millions of Eurail User Records
item.description:
Eduard Kovacs reports: The Netherlands-based company disclosed a data breach in mid-January, informing the public that the personal, order, and travel reservation information of customers who were issued a Eurail pass may have been compromised. Those who reserved a seat through Eurail may also be affected. Eurail said at the time that hackers accessed systems storing basic... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Man arrested for demanding reward after accidental police data leak
item.description:
Dutch authorities arrested a 40-year-old man after he downloaded confidential documents that had been mistakenly shared by the police and refused to delete them unless he received "something in return." [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Washington Hotel in Japan discloses ransomware infection incident
item.description:
The Washington Hotel brand in Japan has announced that that its servers were compromised in a ransomware attack, exposing various business data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Poland arrests suspect linked to Phobos ransomware operation
item.description:
Polish police have detained a 47-year-old man suspected of ties to the Phobos ransomware group and seized computers and mobile phones containing stolen credentials, credit card numbers, and server access data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Chinese hackers exploiting Dell zero-day flaw since mid-2024
item.description:
A suspected Chinese state-backed hacking group has been quietly exploiting a critical Dell security flaw in zero-day attacks that started in mid-2024. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Flaws in popular VSCode extensions expose developers to attacks
item.description:
Vulnerabilities with high to critical severity ratings affecting popular Visual Studio Code (VSCode) extensions collectively downloaded more than 128 million times could be exploited to steal local files and execute code remotely. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Far Bank Enterprises
item.description:
Far Bank Enterprises reported a ransomware. Attributed to INC_RANSOM.
🎭 Threat Actor: INC_RANSOM | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware
